report.summary: A CRITICAL flood event in Peshawar has inundated 153.4 km² across 22 mapped zones, affecting approximately 540,000 people with 91% SAR confidence. Infrastructure impact is severe, with 14 hospitals at risk, 89 km of roads blocked, and 67 schools affected. The most urgent risk is that only one evacuation route remains operational, constraining egress from critical zones FZ-01 and FZ-02. Priorities for the next 6 hours are immediate evacuation of FZ-01/FZ-02, securing Lady Reading Hospital, and establishing traffic control on the remaining route. All figures are satellite-derived estimates requiring ground-truth validation.
report.detailed_body: A critical flood event in Peshawar, Pakistan, has affected 153.37 km², impacting 540,000 people. SAR VV/VH analysis confirms a mean ratio of 0.24, indicating widespread inundation with 24.3% of the area damaged. The disaster map comprises 22 zones, including a critical deep-water zone (FZ-01, 12.4 km²) and a high-severity water zone (FZ-02, 8.7 km²). Critical infrastructure is threatened, with 14 hospitals at risk, 89 km of roads blocked, and 67 schools affected. Only one evacuation route remains available, heightening the emergency.
report.technical_analysis: Sentinel-1 SAR was selected because cloud cover is 42%. The analysis uses SAR VV/VH ratio with mean value 0.24 and identifies 22 zones. Damage is estimated at 24.3%, with 89 km of roads blocked and a vulnerability score of 8.2.
recommendations: Prioritize evacuation in critical flood zones.
Deploy emergency medical support near hospitals at risk.
Clear blocked road corridors for rescue access.
Open temporary shelters for displaced families.
Monitor flood expansion using updated satellite imagery.
Evacuate high-risk zones FZ-01 and FZ-02 immediately
Secure Lady Reading Hospital and deploy medical surge teams
Establish emergency traffic control on the remaining evacuation route
Emergency medical supplies and surge staffing
response_priorities: Activate evacuation support for FZ-01 and FZ-02.
Protect hospital access and stage medical surge teams near high-risk facilities.
Clear blocked road corridors needed for rescue and logistics movement.
assumptions: Satellite, hazard, and impact values are local demo data until upstream agents publish live outputs.
Risk zone geometries are simplified mock polygons for dashboard and report demonstration.
Facility and route coordinates are approximate and intended for prototype visualization.
limitations: No live Band messages are consumed in this local demo run.
No field validation, hydrologic model, or live basemap tiles are used for artifact generation.
Impact estimates should be treated as decision-support indicators, not official counts.

criticality.rationale: Critical flood inundating 153 km² affects 540,000 people, with 14 hospitals at risk, 89 km of roads blocked, and only one evacuation route remaining.

anomalies.description: PDF and Map URLs are empty
anomalies.recommended_handling: Verify report generation pipeline and attach assets

map_narrative.map_narrative: The static risk map identifies critical flood inundation across 153.37 km² of Peshawar, indicating a severe disaster scenario with high population exposure. High-confidence SAR analysis highlights 22 affected zones where critical infrastructure is compromised and evacuation capacity is severely restricted to a single operational route.
key_spatial_findings: 153.37 km² total inundated area across 22 zones
Severe infrastructure impact: 14 hospitals and 67 schools affected
Logistical bottleneck: 89 km of blocked roads with only one active evacuation route
High vulnerability score of 8.2 across affected residential and commercial sectors
map_limitations: Static snapshot not accounting for real-time flood crest movement
SAR data used due to high cloud cover (42%), which may impact resolution in specific urban textures

next_6_hours: Evacuate high-risk zones FZ-01 and FZ-02 immediately
Secure Lady Reading Hospital and deploy medical surge teams
Establish emergency traffic control on the remaining evacuation route
next_24_hours: Clear primary road corridors to restore rescue access
Open and staff temporary shelters for 540,000 affected people
Conduct field validation of satellite inundation maps
next_72_hours: Restore access to 67 affected schools and public buildings
Implement long-term drainage and water removal operations
Assess structural integrity of the 14 high-risk hospitals
resource_priorities: Emergency medical supplies and surge staffing
coordination_priorities: Sync satellite data with local ground-truth reports
Coordinate multi-agency traffic management for evacuation
Align hospital capacity with displaced population needs

decision_brief.decision_brief: CRITICAL flood in Peshawar has inundated 153.4 km² across 22 zones, affecting an estimated 540,000 people (91% SAR confidence). Critical infrastructure exposure is severe: 14 hospitals at risk, 89 km of roads blocked, and 67 schools affected (vulnerability score 8.2). The decisive constraint is that only one evacuation route remains operational, severely limiting egress from critical zones FZ-01 and FZ-02. Immediate action is required to expand evacuation capacity, secure Lady Reading Hospital with medical surge, and clear road corridors for rescue access. Figures are satellite-derived decision-support estimates pending field validation; report PDF/map assets are missing and require pipeline verification.
decision_brief.official_summary: A CRITICAL flood event in Peshawar has inundated 153.4 km² across 22 mapped zones, affecting approximately 540,000 people with 91% SAR confidence. Infrastructure impact is severe, with 14 hospitals at risk, 89 km of roads blocked, and 67 schools affected. The most urgent risk is that only one evacuation route remains operational, constraining egress from critical zones FZ-01 and FZ-02. Priorities for the next 6 hours are immediate evacuation of FZ-01/FZ-02, securing Lady Reading Hospital, and establishing traffic control on the remaining route. All figures are satellite-derived estimates requiring ground-truth validation.
key_decisions_required: Authorize immediate evacuation of critical zones FZ-01 and FZ-02 and approve emergency traffic control on the single operational route
Deploy medical surge teams and secure Lady Reading Hospital and other high-risk hospitals
Commit heavy machinery and crews to clear 89 km of blocked road corridors for rescue access
Open and staff temporary shelters scaled to displaced population needs
Direct field validation of satellite inundation maps and resolve missing report/map assets
